content:
# chrome-remote-debugging-security-boundary - Overview

Automation type: safety_protocol
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP
Goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

When to use:
- Launching Chrome with remote debugging
- Agent controls browser
- Sensitive logged-in websites

Inputs required:
- Port
- Binding address
- Browser profile
- Task scope

AI summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.

# chrome-remote-debugging-security-boundary - Execution Steps

Automation type: safety_protocol
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP
Goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Bind to localhost only.
- Use a dedicated automation profile when possible.
- Avoid leaving remote debugging browser open after task.
- Do not share WebSocket endpoint or expose port through firewall/tunnel.
- Pause before destructive actions, purchases, deletes, or publishing.

AI summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.

# chrome-remote-debugging-security-boundary - Commands or APIs

Automation type: safety_protocol
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP
Goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- --remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1
- netstat / Get-NetTCPConnection to verify listener
- Stop browser/process after task

AI summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.

# chrome-remote-debugging-security-boundary - Verification

Automation type: safety_protocol
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP
Goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Port only listens locally
- No unattended debug browser remains
- Dangerous actions are confirmed

AI summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.

# chrome-remote-debugging-security-boundary - Failure Modes

Automation type: safety_protocol
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP
Goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Debug port exposed
- Wrong website/account controlled
- Credentials visible in logs/screenshots
- Unconfirmed irreversible action

AI summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.

# chrome-remote-debugging-security-boundary - Safety Notes

Automation type: safety_protocol
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP
Goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Treat CDP as high privilege browser control.
- Never expose debug port publicly.
- Do not automate payments/deletion/publishing without explicit confirmation.

AI summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.

# chrome-remote-debugging-security-boundary - Handoff Report

Automation type: safety_protocol
Tooling: Chrome, Edge, CDP
Goal: Prevent remote debugging from becoming a local security risk.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://developer.chrome.com/docs/devtools/remote-debugging

- Debug address
- Profile
- Scope
- Safety stops
- Shutdown evidence

AI summary: CDP is powerful browser control. Bind remote debugging to 127.0.0.1, use dedicated profiles where possible, never expose the WebSocket endpoint, and stop before irreversible actions.
